content.intro: Initial monies include the first month's rent, deposit, and any additional charges. This guide explains how to request and track these payments from your tenants.

sections.title: What are Initial Monies?
sections.content: Initial monies are the upfront payments required before a tenancy begins. This typically includes the first month's rent (or multiple months if rent is paid upfront), the security deposit, and any applicable fees.

steps.title: Open the Tenancy
steps.content: Navigate to Tenancies and select the tenancy you want to request payment for.

steps.title: Click Request Initial Monies
steps.content: Find and click the 'Request Initial Monies' button. This opens the payment configuration modal.

steps.title: Configure Rent Payment
steps.content: Choose between standard first month's rent or rent upfront. If choosing rent upfront, specify the number of months (1-60). Pro-rata is automatically calculated if the move-in date differs from the rent due date.

steps.title: Set Deposit Amount
steps.content: Enter the deposit amount. Note: The maximum deposit is typically 5 weeks' rent for tenancies with annual rent under £50,000.

steps.title: Add Additional Charges
steps.content: If applicable, add any additional charges such as admin fees or inventory fees. Be aware of the Tenant Fees Act restrictions.

steps.title: Review and Send
steps.content: Review the total amount and holding deposit deduction. Click 'Send Request' to email the payment request to the lead tenant.

sections.title: Holding Deposit
sections.content: If a holding deposit was paid during the offer stage, it's automatically deducted from the total initial monies. You can adjust this amount if needed.

sections.title: Pro-rata Calculation
sections.content: Pro-rata rent applies when the move-in date doesn't align with the rent due date. For example, if rent is due on the 1st but the tenant moves in on the 15th, pro-rata covers the remaining days of that month.

sections.title: Tracking Payment Status
sections.content: Once requested, you can track the payment status from the tenancy details page. You'll receive a notification when the tenant confirms they've made the payment, and you can then verify and mark it as received.
